data.description:
# Explore VCS-derived versioning (hatch-vcs / setuptools-scm) as the single source of version truth

Parked design idea (Option E from the api-stability version-marker analysis). Investigate
adopting **VCS-tag-derived versioning** so the version is computed from the git tag at build
time and is NOT hard-coded anywhere — not in `pyproject.toml`, not in `server.json`, not in
docs. `git tag vX.Y.Z` would become the entire version-bump action, eliminating the
multi-file lockstep bump the release currently performs by hand.

## Context

rebar currently hard-codes the version in `pyproject.toml` `[project].version` and mirrors
it into `server.json` (top-level + `packages[].version`), kept in lockstep manually and
asserted by the release workflow's `authorize` version-lockstep guard. rebar builds with
**Hatchling** and already bakes `REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT` provenance via `hatch_build.py`. The
mainstream zero-touch pattern is `hatch-vcs` (a thin wrapper over `setuptools-scm`) which
derives `project.version` from the latest git tag.

## Why this is an idea, not ready-to-implement

Several open design questions must be resolved before committing:
- **server.json is not covered.** `hatch-vcs` sets the Python package version, but the MCP
  `server.json` version (top-level + packages) still needs to be produced/synced from the
  same source — how? (generate it at build time, a small sync step, or a check).
- **Interaction with the new release-authorization workflow.** The `authorize` job asserts
  `version_input == pyproject == server.json`. With a VCS-derived version there is no static
  `pyproject.version` to compare — the lockstep guard's contract changes. And the release is
  dispatched-from-`main` with a `version` input BEFORE any tag exists (the tag is created
  post-publish), which is the opposite ordering from tag-derived versioning. Reconcile these.
- **CI must fetch tags** (`fetch-depth: 0` / `fetch-tags: true`) everywhere the version is
  needed, or the version resolves to a dev/`0.0.0` string (a known setuptools-scm failure
  mode). rebar's build-once/test jobs run without full history in places.
- **Interaction with `REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT` baking** and the build-once artifact-identity flow
  (story 6168) must be checked so provenance stamping still works.
- **Blast radius vs. payoff.** This is a large change to fix what is otherwise a small
  lockstep bump; weigh against simply keeping the current manual bump (now that the drift-prone
  docs marker is removed under Option A).

## Acceptance Criteria (for promoting this idea to designed work)

- [ ] A short design note that resolves: how `server.json` versions are produced/synced from
  the VCS-derived version; how the dispatch-from-`main` `version` input + post-publish tag
  ordering reconciles with tag-derived versioning; and the CI `fetch-tags` requirements.
- [ ] A prototype or spike showing `hatch-vcs` deriving `project.version` from a tag without
  breaking the build-once / `REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT` provenance flow.
- [ ] A go/no-go recommendation with the tradeoff vs. the status quo (manual lockstep bump).

Discovered from the api-stability version-marker design discussion; sibling of the
version-agnostic-marker change (Option A).
data.title: Explore VCS-derived versioning (hatch-vcs/setuptools-scm) as the single source of version truth
